message:
feat(database): harden MySQL client with result limits, URL parsing, abandoned tx drain

Multiple robustness improvements to the MySQL client:

Result set safety:
- Add DEFAULT_MAX_RESULT_ROWS (1M) constant preventing unbounded memory
  growth from runaway SELECTs
- Add set_max_result_rows()/max_result_rows() public API for runtime
  configuration
- Enforce limit during result set parsing with clear error message

URL parsing:
- Percent-decode user and password from connection URLs, handling
  special characters (e.g., @ in passwords encoded as %40)
- Parse query parameters: ssl-mode/sslmode and connect_timeout
- Add hex_nibble() and percent_decode() helper functions

Packet safety:
- Add assertion in build_packet() preventing payload from exceeding
  MAX_PACKET_SIZE (16 MiB - 1) with clear panic message noting that
  large-payload splitting is not yet implemented

Abandoned transaction handling:
- MySqlTransaction::drop now sets needs_rollback flag on the connection
  instead of silently hoping the server auto-rollbacks
- drain_abandoned_transaction(): issues implicit ROLLBACK on next
  execute() call if a transaction was dropped without commit/rollback
- execute() now calls drain_abandoned_transaction() before sending
  COM_QUERY, ensuring clean connection state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
